absorbed_branches.summary: Integrated reusable Tushare moneyflow inputs, moneyflow technical combo factors, alpha factory gates, walk-forward validation support, archive replay, and tests into core research code. Local data and generated reports remain excluded.

Absorbed the office desktop incremental moneyflow audit and kept large_minus_liquidity_20 in the strict walk-forward validation candidate set. Research evidence remains documentation/config only; local data and generated reports remain excluded.

Absorbed the continued office desktop mining notes and long-sample recheck. The added evidence keeps large_minus_liquidity_20 in strict validation only and explicitly blocks promotion until drawdown, capacity, and RankIC issues are resolved.

Absorbed the combined width and cost audit for large_minus_liquidity_20. Wider portfolios reduce participation pressure but do not solve drawdown and cost sensitivity, so the candidate remains strict-validation observation only.

Absorbed continued combined rejection audits for large_minus_liquidity_20, raw moneyflow priority factors, and mf_low_minus_volatility_20. The evidence narrows future mining toward liquidity-gated moneyflow-risk blends and does not promote any factor.

Absorbed the micro-capacity sensitivity audit. Reducing portfolio value to 100,000 and widening top-N portfolios reduced participation pressure but did not preserve relative return or drawdown control, so raw moneyflow top-N widening remains rejected.

Absorbed the same-sample technical baseline control. Momentum, reversal, liquidity, and risk-adjusted momentum baselines were all rejected, reinforcing that the next useful direction is liquidity-aware or residualized factor design rather than raw top-N searches.

Absorbed the holding-period sensitivity audit. Five-day forward returns and five-day rebalancing did not rescue mf_low_minus_volatility_20 or large_minus_liquidity_20, so holding-period sweeps remain rejected and the next useful direction stays liquidity-aware factor construction.

Absorbed the failure attribution audit. large_minus_liquidity_20 failed mainly through severe 2024 drawdown and non-monotonic quantiles rather than capacity, while mf_low_minus_volatility_20 remains capacity-constrained and non-monotonic. Future batches should gate thin names, residualize risk/liquidity, or explicitly target mid-quantile/tail behavior.

Absorbed the directional and signal-date amount-gate probe audit. Simple inversion, percentile targeting, and standalone amount gating were rejected; the next useful factor design should combine liquidity gates with a new score rather than post-filtering an unstable score.

Absorbed the residualized liquidity-aware temporary probe and promoted its reusable design into pre-registered core factor code plus strict multi-regime walk-forward validation. The result remains research-only and not approved for live trading.

Absorbed the office production residual matrix recheck. The production factor builder reproduced the temporary residual probe, keeping large_resid_liq_vol_amt_gate_20 top5 with regime150/180/252 in strict validation while reinforcing blockers for single-lookback regime dependence, regime-filter blocked windows, unstable split IC, and the need for a cached factor-matrix validation path.

Absorbed the residual gate amount-band and rank-window probe. The probe rejected amount-band tweaks and rank offsets as better candidates, and promoted the useful method lesson into core validation: actual selected-holdings tail IC must be reported and gateable because full-universe IC can look significant while the traded tail is not.
